title: The Self-Driven Child — Chapter-by-Chapter Summary
description: A narrated chapter-by-chapter walkthrough of The Self-Driven Child by William Stixrud and Ned Johnson. Covers all fifteen chapters: locus of control and the anxiety epidemic, chronic stress and the brain, parent as consultant, kids as decision makers, the nonanxious presence, intrinsic motivation, radical downtime, sleep, schooling and autonomy, screens, exercise, special needs and autonomy, standardized tests, college readiness, and alternate routes to a meaningful life.

title: Alison Gopnik on The Gardener and the Carpenter
description: Alison Gopnik joins the show to discuss her book The Gardener and the Carpenter. We cover the carpenter vs gardener metaphor, why human childhood is so long, the exploration/exploitation trade-off, alloparenting and the grandmother hypothesis, the biology of parental love, kids as Bayesian scientists, pretend play as counterfactual reasoning, the late-frontal-lobe IQ finding, schools and apprenticeship, screens, and the policy case for treating childhood as a public good.

title: The Gardener and the Carpenter — Alison Gopnik
description: Two hosts break down Alison Gopnik's argument that 'parenting' is the wrong model: kids aren't chairs you sculpt, they're gardens you tend. Covers the carpenter vs gardener metaphor, alloparenting, why pretend play is counterfactual reasoning, and why later frontal-lobe maturation correlates with higher IQ.
